## Cascade efficiency

$$\eta_\text{Carnot}^{T1} = 1 - \frac{298}{535} = 0.4430$$

$$\eta_\text{cascade} = \eta_\text{opt} \cdot \eta_\text{ORC} \cdot \eta_\text{elec} = 0.7000 \times 0.1500 \times 0.6800 = 0.07140$$

## Round-trip efficiencies

| Storage form | $\eta_\text{chg}$ | $\eta_\text{dis}$ | RTE | Extraction $\Sigma_g$ terms |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Pressurised water | 0.9500 | 0.9000 | 0.8550 | Pump and valve wear; no conversion |
| NaAlH₄ → H₂ | 0.8000 | 0.7000 | 0.5600 | Dehydriding vessel; gas regulator; Ti catalyst (cycle consumption) |
| Na-ion battery | 0.9200 | 0.9200 | 0.8464 | Cell capacity fade per cycle; no moving parts |
| Pumped hydro | 0.8000 | 0.8500 | 0.6800 | Turbine and pump maintenance; civil structure is passive |
| NH₃ synthesis→cracking | 0.6000 | 0.7500 | 0.4500 | Cracking reactor at T2; Fe/K catalyst (borderline $\mathcal{E}^*$) |

## Full chain efficiency

| Path | $\eta$ product | Dominant loss step | Storable? | $\mathcal{E}^*$ confirmed? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Solar → CPC → hot water (co-located) | $0.7000 \times 0.9500 = 0.6650$ | Optical collection | No (diurnal only) | Yes |
| Solar → CPC → pressurised storage → hot water | $0.7000 \times 0.8550 \times 0.9500 = 0.5686$ | Storage standby loss | Diurnal | Yes |
| Solar → CPC → ORC → Na-ion → electric motor | $0.7000 \times 0.1500 \times 0.8464 \times 0.9000 = 0.07998$ | Carnot at T1 | Yes | Candidate |
| Solar → CPC → ORC → electrolysis → NaAlH₄ → H₂ → combustion | $0.7000 \times 0.1500 \times 0.6800 \times 0.5600 \times 0.9000 = 0.03599$ | Carnot + RTE | Diurnal–seasonal | Candidate (Ti gate) |
| Solar → CPC → district heating → terminal HX | $0.7000 \times 0.9300^2 \times 0.9500 = 0.5752$ | Distribution standby | Diurnal (pressurised) | Yes (pipe material borderline) |

## Geographic DNI scaling

Reference DNI: 2000 kWh/m²/yr  |  $\eta_\text{opt}$ = 0.7000  |  $E_\text{demand}$ = 8000 kWh/yr

| Location | DNI (kWh/m²/yr) | $A_\text{required}$ (m²) | $\Sigma_g$ multiplier |
| :--- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Atacama | 2900 | 3.941 | 0.6897 |
| Tucson AZ | 2500 | 4.571 | 0.8000 |
| Portland OR | 900 | 12.70 | 2.222 |
| N. Europe | 1000 | 11.43 | 2.000 |
| Polar avg | 600 | 19.05 | 3.333 |

Polar floor ( $\text{DNI} = 400$ kWh/m²/yr): $A = 28.57$ m²

## Location comparison apertures

| Location | DNI (kWh/m²/yr) | $A_\text{required}$ (m²) | $\Sigma_g$ multiplier vs ref |
| :--- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| Tucson AZ | 2500 | 4.571 | 0.8000 |
| Portland OR | 900 | 12.70 | 2.222 |

Portland/Tucson aperture ratio: $2.778\times$

## Tucson worked trace

**Step 1 — Aperture area**

$$A = \frac{8000}{0.7000 \times 2500} = 4.571\ \text{m}^2$$

**Step 2 — Al mass**

$$m_\text{Al,reflector} = 4.571 \times 0.0015 \times 2700 = 18.51\ \text{kg}$$

$$m_\text{Al,total} = 3 \times 18.51 = 55.54\ \text{kg}$$

**Step 3 — $\Sigma_{g,\text{mfg}}^{\text{CPC}}$**

$$W_\text{irrev,fab} = 55.54\ \text{kg} \times 2\ \text{MJ/kg} = 111.1\ \text{MJ}$$

$$\Sigma_{g,\text{mfg}}^{\text{CPC}} = \frac{111.1 \times 10^6}{298} = 372.8\ \text{kJ/K}$$

**Step 4 — Storage vessel**

Daily demand: $21.92$ kWh/day. Buffer (14 hr): $12.79$ kWh. $\Delta T = 55$ K. Energy density $= 0.06380$ kWh/litre.

$$V_\text{tank} = \frac{12.79}{0.06380} = 200.4\ \text{litres}$$

$$m_\text{Al,tank} = 0.15\ \text{m}^2 \times 0.002\ \text{m} \times 2700 = 0.8100\ \text{kg}$$

$$\Sigma_{g,\text{mfg}}^{\text{storage}} = 5.436\ \text{kJ/K}$$

**Step 5 — Terminal heat exchanger**

$$\Sigma_{g,\text{mfg}}^{\text{terminal}} = \frac{0.5\ \text{kg} \times 2\ \text{MJ/kg} \times 10^6}{298} = 3.356\ \text{kJ/K}$$

**Step 6 — Full chain $\Sigma_g^{\text{full}}$**

$$\Sigma_g^{\text{full}} = 372.8 + 5.436 + 3.356 = 381.6\ \text{kJ/K}$$

CPC term: 97.70% of total.

**Step 7 — $\Sigma_s^{\text{annual}}$ and $\tau^*$**

$$\Sigma_s^{\text{annual}} = \frac{8000 \times 3.6 \times 10^6}{0.85 \times 1700} = 19.93\ \text{MJ/K/yr}$$

$$\tau^* = \frac{381.6\ \text{kJ/K}}{19.93\ \text{MJ/K/yr}} = 0.01914\ \text{yr} = 6.988\ \text{days}$$

Dominance margin: $522.3\times$ (10 yr) — $2612.\times$ (50 yr).
